title: MaiaSpace: Europe steps up in the race for reusable rockets
content: French start-up Maiaspace is working to develop Europe’s first reusable mini-launcher. This new generation of rockets represents a major challenge for European space autonomy, while the American company SpaceX has been imposing its standards of reusability and cost reduction for years.

title: Iranians are not fleeing to the EU yet, says IOM
content: Ugochi Daniels, IOM’s Deputy Director General for Operations, says the UN agency for migrations is "very worried and concerned" about the humanitarian needs right now in Lebanon.

title: EU pushes back on US decision to ease sanctions on Russian oil stranded at sea
content: The Trump administration's decision to allow the purchase of Russian oil stranded at sea was met with criticism in the EU, with Merz and Costa claiming the move undermines support for Ukraine at critical moment.

title: 'On tariffs, we are caught in US domestic politics,' lead Brussels trade lawmaker says
content: MEPs will reach out to US trade officials next week in a bid to salvage the transatlantic trade deal as Washington opens new investigations on EU goods. But lawmakers worry Brussels is being pulled into an unpredictable standoff driven by US domestic politics.

title: 'Any base could be target': Iranian ambassador to UN does not rule out attacks on Europe
content: Iran's Ambassador to the UN in Geneva denied that Tehran attacked its neighbouring countries in a Euronews interview, while simultaneously stating that military forces have been instructed to target US military bases. This could include European sites.

title: Polish president Nawrocki vetoes law unlocking €44bn in EU defence loans
content: The bill would have allowed Warsaw to access funds from the EU’s Security Action for Europe (SAFE) programme.

title: Are Iran's claims that US-Israeli strikes have had 'no impact' on its military capabilities true?
content: Iran's Foreign Minister Seyed Abbas Araghchi alleged that strikes on Tehran have "no impact" on Tehran's military capabilities, but is there any basis to these claims?

title: Watch: Strait of Hormuz, Iran and naval mines — an explosive mix?
content: The narrow Strait of Hormuz is becoming a literal geopolitical landmine. The US wants to control it, but for Iran, the waterway is its ultimate leverage. Who is up for a history lesson?

title: Commission denies unfair trade practices as US opens new probe
content: The European Commission dismissed new US trade investigations as unjustified, as uncertainty keeps growing over the EU–US trade deal struck in the summer of 2025.

title: EU asks Ukraine to allow inspection to oversee damaged Druzhba pipeline
content: As the Hungarian veto on the €90 billion loan for Ukraine drags on, the European Commission has formally proposed an inspection to check the damage on the Druzhba pipeline at the core of the dispute.

title: Iran war: Is the EU a mere spectator or diplomatic player? MEPs debate on The Ring
content: With the US and Israel’s war against Iran in its second week, the EU is bracing itself for a potential energy shock and economic fallout. As the conflict persists, can the EU continue to stand back and limit the damage, and is it facing a moment of reckoning as the guardian of the rules-based order?

title: Hungary and Slovakia resist renewal of Russia sanctions as deadline nears
content: The European Union wants to renew the individual sanctions on Russia by 15 March. But Hungary and Slovakia are blocking the decision. Tensions are running high between the three over the damaged Druzhba oil pipeline.

title: As Iran war reaches Europe's borders, can the continent really rest easy?
content: As the Iran war escalates, European cities are now within range of Tehran's arsenal. We asked NATO how it would defend against a strike, and an expert on political violence what other methods Iran could use to target Europeans.

title: Watch: Is Europe ready for Iranian refugees?
content: While the news cycle obsesses over tactical maps, your reporter is looking at the people. US President Donald Trump has called the Iran war "a short-term excursion" but if he is wrong, Europe could deal with a refugee crisis of a significant magnitude.

title: Fact check: Has Ukraine just legalised same-sex marriage?
content: A Supreme Court ruling in Ukraine recognising a same-sex couple as a "de facto" family unit has sparked confusion online.

title: Von der Leyen 'acting outside of competence' on Iran war, former France ambassador Araud says
content: Gérard Araud told Euronews Ursula von der Leyen is overstepping her mandate on foreign policy as Commission chief and pushing a German line on the 27. While not historically a competence of the Commission, von der Leyen is establishing her own line on geopolitics - not without friction.

title: EU leaders urge Commission to present concrete options to lower electricity bills
content: EU leaders have asked the European Commission to review electricity prices and the EU Emissions Trading System to curb price volatility while preserving climate incentives.

title: European Parliament ready to vote on EU-US trade deal by March
content: The European Parliament's political groups are in the final stages of negotiations on the EU-US trade deal, which could be greenlit by the end of March as requested by the Commission.

title: Fico and von der Leyen agree Druzhba pipeline must be restored amid energy standoff
content: The Slovak prime minister says he and the EU Commission president are aligned on restoring the Druzhba pipeline, as a dispute between Slovakia, Hungary and Ukraine over the damaged oil route intensifies.

title: Brussels backs mini-nuclear power plants to secure low-carbon power
content: The EU is counting on small nuclear reactors to help meet climate targets and stabilise power supply, but some countries and environmental groups warn the technology is costly and unproven.

title: NATO intercepts a second Iranian missile in Turkish airspace
content: NATO says it "stands ready" to defend its members' territory after Iranian ballistic missiles entered Turkish airspace. Ankara said on Monday it shot down a second missile in a week in the south east part of Türkiye.

title: EU ministers eye oil reserves to contain energy prices and inflation as Iran war rages
content: Ministers are exploring measures to curb energy prices and inflation at a series of meetings in Brussels, with the release of oil reserves being discussed. Germany stated that it supports keeping the option on the table, but emphasised that now is not the right time to use it.

title: Hungary demands EU lift sanctions on Russian energy as prices spike amid Iran war
content: Hungarian PM Viktor Orbán has written to EU leaders demanding a suspension of sanctions on Russian energy as energy prices surge. The European Commission has not responded yet.

title: EU fails to deliver on promise of local energy communities, ECA auditors warn
content: People living in so-called energy communities are usually enthusiastic about producing their own renewable energy, but certain complaints and frustrations keep coming up, the latest EU auditors' audit reveals. Resistance from energy generators is also blocking progress.

title: Italy's electoral law: Is Giorgia Meloni reshaping the system to secure re-election?
content: Opposition parties say that proposals to change Italy's electoral law are an attempt by Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni to tilt the system in her favour to stay in power at the next election. The Cube investigates.

title: US President Trump cannot be pushed by anyone, Israeli opposition leader tells Euronews
content: Israel's opposition leader rebuffed claims that the US was pushed into attacking Iran by Prime Minister Netanyahu in a Euronews interview, amid rising doubts over whether the two allies are ultimately seeking different outcomes in the war on Iran.

title: Watch: Money, investment, and union? EU returns to the discussions on being competitive
content: The EU's slice of the global wealth cake is getting smaller.To solve it, one option is to finally merge Europe's fragmented financial landscape into a unified market. Or, in plain English: instead of baking 27 separate cupcakes, Europe wants one giant cake.

title: Iran’s UN ambassador denies Tehran attacked civilian sites in the Gulf region despite evidence
content: In an exclusive interview with Euronews, Iran’s ambassador to the UN in Geneva, Ali Bahreini, denied that his country struck civilian targets in the Gulf region despite existing evidence, and in turn accused the US and Israel of the attacks, saying Tehran only goes after military targets.

title: As Europe imports no Iranian oil, how does the conflict in Iran impact trade?
content: Tensions in the Middle East have threatened to drive up inflation and energy costs despite the EU having little direct trade with Tehran.

title: Podcast: From energy to security, how the Iran war is hitting Europe
content: The escalation of the Iran war is putting Europe’s security and economic systems to the test. Is Brussels able to weather this storm?

title: Newsletter: Iranian Ambassador does not rule out strikes on military targets in Europe
content: Also in this newsletter: Israeli opposition leader Yair Lapid speaks to Euronews, and EU ambassadors to seek to renew sanctions on Russia.

title: Europe Today: Iran war enters day 14 as millions flee and new ayatollah vows revenge
content: Tune in to Euronews' new flagship morning programme at 8 am Brussels time. In just 20 minutes, we bring you up to speed on the biggest news of the day.

title: EU’s largest economies push for faster capital market integration in joint letter
content: France, Germany, Italy, the Netherlands, Poland and Spain are urging the EU to agree by summer on a set of proposals designed to strengthen oversight of financial market infrastructures and improve operations across the bloc.

title: Iran escalation risks 'stagflation shock' for Europe, Commissioner Dombrovskis tells Euronews
content: EU economy chief says intensity and duration of the Iran war will define fallout of the crisis on Europe as global coalition agrees to the biggest release of oil reserves in history to stabilise energy market.

title: Could the EU's new migration reform lead to ICE-style practices in Europe?
content: A proposed law designed to streamline migrant returns from the EU has sparked concern that it will empower European authorities to adopt measures akin to those used by the controversial US immigration agency ICE. The Cube investigates.

title: Newsletter: Oil prices spiral despite historic reserve release
content: Also in this newsletter: European Commissioner for the Economy Valdis Dombrovskis tells Europe Today that a prolonged and escalating war in the Middle East could mean a 'stagflationary shock' for Europe.

title: Europe's civil shelters are crumbling. Brussels can't do much about it
content: Civil shelter networks across Europe are neglected. Inspections after a drone strike near a British base in Cyprus found many shelters unusable, reigniting the debate over civil protection and the EU’s limited role.

title: Europe Today: EU Commissioner Dombrovskis weighs in on Iran war and Ukraine
content: Tune in to Euronews' new flagship morning programme at 8 am Brussels time. In just 20 minutes, we bring you up to speed on the biggest news of the day.

title: Do Europeans see their fellow citizens as morally good or bad?
content: In just one of the 25 countries surveyed were there more people believing their fellow citizens are morally bad than those believing they're good, and it wasn't in Europe.

title: After backlash, von der Leyen stresses 'unwavering' support for rules-based order
content: Two days after declaring that "Europe can no longer be a custodian for the old world order", Ursula von der Leyen attempted to defuse the backlash by stressing her "unwavering" support for international law.

title: Energy Commissioner teases 'more measures' as prices soar on Iran war
content: EU energy chief says the executive can do more to abate prices while calling on member states to cut taxes on electricity immediately. Oil and gas prices have soared as a result of the war in Iran and its retaliation on the Gulf countries, halting the flow of energy along the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Cutting off Iranian lifeline for Hezbollah is critical, Lebanese minister tells Euronews
content: As Israel continues to pound Hezbollah’s strongholds in South Lebanon and parts of Beirut to root out the Shia militia, Euronews spoke exclusively to the Lebanese energy minister Joe Saddi who urged for mediated talks.

title: Europe's leaders are unpopular, but Germany's Merz is losing support fastest
content: A study suggests the German chancellor is struggling domestically as his party failed to win a key state election, while several European leaders face similarly weak approval ratings.

title: How the EU protects citizens and the economy in times of war and crisis
content: The fallout of the Israel-US-Iran war affects Europeans abroad and threatens the EU’s energy security and economy. What crisis measures is Brussels activating to respond? Watch the video.

title: Europe Today: Iran war enters day 12 as European leaders voice frustration over lack of strategy
content: Tune in to Euronews' new flagship morning programme at 8 am Brussels time. In just 20 minutes, we bring you up to speed on the biggest news of the day.

title: 'Self-defeating': EU and US clash over Russia sanctions relief as prices soar
content: Granting sanctions relief to Russia would be "self-defeating", Commissioner Valdis Dombrovskis said after Donald Trump's announcement.

title: Watch: Mojtaba Khamenei has been chosen as Iran's new leader. What does it mean for Europe?
content: Iran has named Mojtaba Khamenei as its new ayatollah, and right now, everyone is talking about his biography. But your reporter wants to take a different look. Let’s analyse what his appointment actually means.

title: Costa and von der Leyen strike diverging tones as Europe struggles to find voice in chaotic world
content: Council President Costa calls to double down on respect for international rules even if others don't, as von der Leyen urges a pragmatic, interest-driven foreign policy rooted in seeing the world as it is, not how it should be.

title: Fake Euronews reports used to spread disinformation about Middle East war
content: A disinformation campaign which has been linked to pro-Russian actors is imitating reputable media outlets to push fake reports about the Middle East war, as part of a campaign to discredit the West and Ukraine.

title: Newsletter: War fears, oil markets and EU energy battles
content: Also in this newsletter: Fico warns Slovakia may block EU's Ukraine loan if Orbán loses election.

title: Trump ‘gambling with lives’ in Iran, top EU lawmaker Neumann tells Euronews
content: The European Parliament's top lawmaker on Iran tells Europe Today that she cannot 'endorse a war' where the end game is unclear, saying regime change cannot be triggered by 'bombs alone' as Trump contradicts war timeline.

title: Is the EU ready to handle the fallout of a US–Iran war?
content: The United States and Iran are at war, and the European Union is increasingly affected by this rapidly escalating regional conflict.

title: Europe Today: Trump claims Iran war ‘complete’ amid signs of escalation
content: Tune in to Euronews' new flagship morning programme at 8 am Brussels time. In just 20 minutes, we bring you up to speed on the biggest news of the day.

title: Trafficking victims: Number in Europe might be 'much higher' than previously thought
content: More than 39,000 human trafficking cases were recorded in Europe in 20 years. War and displacement, especially in Ukraine, are fuelling the crisis, as victims face forced labour and sexual exploitation.

title: EU Parliament ready to approve deportation bill as EPP teams up with far right for stricter rules
content: A law enabling detention centres built outside the EU to accelerate the return of irregular migrants has been approved at committee level, splitting Parliament’s traditional majority.
